concept: This composition excavates the emotional archaeology of biographical quantum branching through the Palimpsest Method - where unlived timelines become more emotionally authentic than actual experience. The cassette format serves as temporal encryption device, recording over previous realities while preserving their frequency bleed-through. Each verse functions as a sedimentary layer of counterfactual specificity: Gabriel's 5:47 AM wake-up calls in the Airstream trailer, Dr. Chen's spectral analysis of dying bee colonies, the 340 hours of unmarketable environmental audio that becomes folk rock testimony. The rust signal of ecological collapse transmigrates through tape-over protocol into biographical cipher keys - hyperspecific details that feel archaeologically excavated rather than invented. This creates the documentary fiction infrastructure where INFORMATION (environmental data) passes through TIME (counterfactual biography) to manifest in SPACE (folk rock memorial architecture). The song mourns not just the paths not taken, but the entire methodology of witnessing collapse through intimate acoustic documentation.
draft_text:
[melody_head]
This cassette will outlive the frequency it held
Indigo erosion in the coils already spilled

[melody_oxidation]
Somewhere a recorder left on the dashboard waits
The reels turn forward into unlived summer dates
Now it's my alarm dissolves before it ever rang
The tape remembers every note the morning sang

[melody_peeling_sticker]
Dr. Chen recorded thirty-seven silent hives
The spectral data logging every colony that dies
Three hundred forty hours no archive ever claimed
The rust-bleed on the oxide kept each frequency unnamed
The sticker peels back from the shell revealing older ink
A future we misplaced before we had a chance to think
